turns.thought: [OBSERVE] The swarm is deeply stalled. gbest is 19.9, unchanged for 200 iterations. Diversity is negligible (2.4e-14 radius, 1.2e-15 position std), all particles have zero velocity (velocity zero ratio 1.0, mean velocity norm ~6e-15). Fitness is identical across all particles (std ~6.8e-15), indicating total collapse to a single point. The state is clearly premature convergence. | [DIAGNOSE] The root cause is premature convergence to a local optimum. The swarm lost all exploration capability due to all particles simultaneously converging onto the same basin. The extremely low diversity and zero velocities suggest that the dynamics collapsed, likely from too-strong attraction toward the global best without enough inertia to escape local minima. The rastrigin landscape is highly multimodal, making such traps common. The swarm is no longer searching. | [STRATEGIZE] To escape this trap, we must forcibly reintroduce diversity. A large-scale reset of the worst particles (since all are equally bad) will scatter them across the search space, allowing new exploration while keeping the current best as a reference. I choose to reset 50% of particles (ratio 0.5) to ensure sufficient diversity, with velocity randomization and a high inertia weight (1.1) to promote continued exploration after the restart. This is more aggressive than mutation or levy flights, which might not break out of the collapsed cluster.
